summary: Matched and linked the Sheik needle thrown item TU by changing itseakneedlethrown.c source shape, marking the object Matching in configure.py, and adjusting GALE01 symbol metadata for local sdata/sdata2 literals. The bot report recorded new matches for the unit's .data, .sdata2, and two collision functions.

key_files.role: Primary item TU source changes for matching/linking: state table callbacks, include changes, local ordering and stack tactics, collision function adjustments, and inline/static signature change.
evidence_refs: text_corpus:decomp-dev bot report new matches for itseakneedlethrown .data, .sdata2, itSeakneedlethrown_UnkMotion0_Coll, itSeakneedlethrown_UnkMotion1_Coll

key_files.role: Build configuration changed itseakneedlethrown.c from NonMatching to Matching.
Symbol metadata adjusted several sdata and sdata2 literals from global it_ address names to local @ labels.

what_changed.text: Filled `ItemStateTable it_803F6F50` entries with the motion-specific Anim/Phys/Coll callbacks instead of NULL callbacks.
Replaced include `it/it_266F.h` with `it/itgroundcoll.h` in the item TU.
Added a small `sdata2_order` helper referencing `0.0f`, `1.0f`, and `-1.0f`, apparently to influence local sdata2 ordering.
Changed local declaration order in `itSeakNeedleThrown_CheckGroundHit`, moving `u32 line_id` before `Vec3 hit_pos`.
Changed two collision functions to get the item via `gobj->user_data` instead of `GET_ITEM(gobj)`.
Moved `itSeakneedlethrown_UnkMotion3_Coll` lower in the file, after `itSeakneedlethrown_UnkMotion2_Coll`.
Converted selected GALE01 sdata and sdata2 literal symbols from global `it_804D...` names to local `@...` labels.

smart_moves.text: The PR linked the object only after source and symbol metadata changes produced bot-reported new matches for the item unit.
The PR treated literal ownership/visibility as part of the match by changing address-named global literal symbols to local labels in symbols.txt.
The item state table was made explicit with callback functions, which coincided with the bot report showing `.data` reaching 100% for the unit.

decomp_lessons.lesson: For item TUs, a complete `ItemStateTable` with real motion Anim/Phys/Coll callbacks can be a decisive data-section matching step when the previous table has NULL callbacks.
Literal symbol scope changes in `symbols.txt` may be required for item TU data matching, but they should be grounded in section ownership/metadata rather than treated as general source style.

Very small source-shape edits such as local declaration order, direct `gobj->user_data`, inline linkage, and narrow `PAD_STACK` use can close final percent gaps, but they need verifier evidence and should not become broad style.
evidence_refs: text_corpus:bot report new matches for UnkMotion0_Coll and UnkMotion1_Coll

naming_conventions.text: This PR retained existing address-style names such as `it_803F6F50`, `it_803F6FA0`, and `it_803F7060`; no semantic global renames were introduced in the diff.
The symbol metadata changes prefer local `@NNN` labels over global `it_804D...` names for selected local literals.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Object status flip from NonMatching to Matching after source and symbol changes.
evidence_refs: text_corpus:bot report matched/linked code and data improvements

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Use explicit state table callback references to match the item unit's data table.
Adjust local sdata/sdata2 symbol labels from global address-style symbols to local labels to match data ownership/placement.
Introduce `sdata2_order` with float references, apparently to affect sdata2 ordering.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caution: The PR evidence does not explain why this helper was necessary; global standards treat data-order helpers as high-risk unless required and verified.
Keep PAD_STACK evidence-tied; do not generalize as preferred style.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Change local variable declaration order in a ground-hit helper.

evidence_refs: text_corpus:empty PR body plus decomp-dev bot report
notes: The diff and bot report provide concrete evidence for changed files and reported matching improvements.
The PR body is empty and there are no review comments, so author rationale and reviewer intent are unavailable.
No independent build, objdiff, or regression output beyond the decomp-dev bot report is present in the slice.
The purpose of `sdata2_order` and `PAD_STACK(4)` is inferred from placement and bot-reported match changes, not explained by the author.

accepted_candidate_records.title: PR 2663 linked `itseakneedlethrown.c` with item state table, symbol metadata, and narrow source-shape edits
accepted_candidate_records.text: In PR 2663, `melee/it/items/itseakneedlethrown.c` was changed from NonMatching to Matching. The diff filled the item state table with real callbacks, localized selected sdata/sdata2 symbols in `config/GALE01/symbols.txt`, changed small source shapes including `gobj->user_data`, local declaration order, `PAD_STACK(4)`, and inline linkage, and the bot report recorded new matches for `.data`, `.sdata2`, `itSeakneedlethrown_UnkMotion0_Coll`, and `itSeakneedlethrown_UnkMotion1_Coll`.

accepted_candidate_records.title: PAD_STACK and data-order helpers appeared in a final matching PR but require narrow evidence
accepted_candidate_records.text: This PR used `PAD_STACK(4)` and a `sdata2_order` helper while closing final match gaps. The evidence supports that they were part of the landed diff and coincided with bot-reported matches, but the PR does not explain necessity. Treat as a cautionary case aligned with the standard that matching tactics need explicit verifier evidence.

source_update_candidates.title: `itseakneedlethrown.c` marked Matching in PR 2663
source_update_candidates.text: PR 2663 changed `Object(NonMatching, "melee/it/items/itseakneedlethrown.c")` to `Object(Matching, "melee/it/items/itseakneedlethrown.c")` in `configure.py`; the bot report recorded new matches for this item unit.
source_update_candidates.reason: Candidate path/status fact for curator review; intake should not directly promote matching status into source facts.

source_update_candidates.title: Selected `itseakneedlethrown` sdata/sdata2 literals became local labels
source_update_candidates.text: PR 2663 changed selected GALE01 `.sdata` and `.sdata2` symbols near `it_804D5458` and `it_804DCF60` from global address-style names to local `@...` labels.
source_update_candidates.reason: Candidate symbol/path metadata fact; verify ownership in current source before promoting.

source_update_candidates.title: Clarify risk around helper functions used only for data ordering
source_update_candidates.text: This PR added `static void sdata2_order(void)` with float literal references while matching/linking an item TU. Consider whether standards should explicitly call out such helpers as data-order tactics that require narrow evidence and cleanup review.
source_update_candidates.reason: Existing standards cover fake data anchors and tactic evidence, but curator may want a more searchable note for source helpers that exist only to force literal order.

rejection_notes: Do not infer that `gobj->user_data` should replace `GET_ITEM` generally; the evidence only shows two local changes in this PR.
Do not infer a general approval of `PAD_STACK`; the PR has no reviewer explanation and standards require explicit verifier evidence.
Do not infer that adding `sdata2_order` helpers is accepted normal style; the PR evidence only shows this instance and the standards caution against data-order work without ownership evidence.
No review feedback is recorded in the slice, so no reviewer intent should be attributed.
